steps.text: Choose a mathematical technique for analyzing spectral data by comparison with reference spectra.
Use a sufficient number (>3) of reference spectra in the bracketed range to demonstrate linearity in that concentration range.
Select specific wavelengths in each region where the target analyte does not overlap with an interfering compound.
Follow the procedures specified in section 11.1 to conduct the leak check.
Follow the procedures specified in section 11.2 to verify detector linearity.
Follow the procedures specified in section 11.8 for data storage requirements.
Flow dry nitrogen through the gas cell and verify that no significant amounts of absorbing species are present.
Collect a background spectrum using a signal averaging period equal to or longer than that being used for averaging of source sample spectra.
Flow the CTS gas through the cell and verify that the measured concentration is stable to within the uncertainty of the gas standard.
Record the spectrum for the CTS gas.
Conduct a QA spike per the instructions in section 11.4 of this method.
While sampling, monitor the signal transmittance and obtain a new background spectrum if the transmittance changes by 5% or more.
Record another CTS spectrum after the sampling run.
Perform post-run QA per section 9.1.2 of this method.
Perform spiking after each run.
Inject zero air into the system.
Measure the native stack concentration of the species to be spiked.
Determine the dilution factor (DF) for each dynamic spike.
Calculate the standard addition response (SAR).
Obtain reference spectra for each analyte, interferant, surrogate, CTS, and tracer.

Report traceability and other pertinent information for each reference spectrum, for each compound, including: temperature, pressure, concentration, cylinder source and specifications, spectral regions of analysis used for quantitation, and calibration fit equations and correlations.

If commercially prepared, or other available reference libraries are used to quantify data, ensure the FTIR spectral resolution and line position, cell pathlength, temperature and pressure, and apodization function are known and reported.
Ensure resolution, line position, and apodization function used for collection of sample spectra are the same as those of the reference spectra used for quantitation.
Ensure reference spectra for each target compound bracket the concentration of that compound in the sample stream.
In the case where traceable reference spectra provided by the FTIR manufacturer do not bracket the concentration of a particular compound, take necessary actions.
Seal the probe end of the system by capping or plugging the end of the sample probe.
Start sampling pumps and operate them until the pressure stabilizes.
Observe/measure the flow through the vacuum-side of the sampling system. A flow of less than 0.5% of the system’s normal in-use flow rate is acceptable.
Seal the probe end of the system as close to the probe opening as possible by capping or plugging the end of the sample probe.
Operate all vacuum pumps.
Draw a vacuum on the sampling system and let the pressure on the system stabilize.
Turn off the sample pumps and seal the system under a vacuum of 250 mmHg greater than the source static pressure.
Record the absolute pressure and the system absolute temperature every 30 seconds for 5 minutes. The leak rate must be equal to or less than 2.5 mmHg per minute.

Perform testing at a single point that most closely matches the mean if the concentration at each traverse point differs from the mean concentration for all traverse points by no more than 5.0% of the mean concentration.
Sample using the '3-point short line' if the concentration at each traverse point differs from the mean concentration by no more than 10% of the mean.
Sample using the stratification check procedure specified in section 11.5.1.1 if the concentration at any traverse point differs from the mean by more than 10%.
Assign a unique filename to each spectrum and separately to each corresponding interferogram. Spectra and interferograms must be providable in '.spc' format upon request.
Measure the temperature of the gas cell directly.
Calibrate the temperature measurement device to within ±0.1 °C every 12 months.
Measure the gas cell pressure empirically.
Calibrate the measurement device to within ±1 mmHg every 12 months.

preconditions.text: If the transmittance (relative to background) changes by 5% or more in any analytical spectral region, obtain a new background spectrum.
If spiking is not performed after each run and the post-test spike fails, all data for that test are invalid.
Use consistent concentration units for each variable in the equations for dilution factor calculations.
The criteria for single point sampling is not met if the concentration at each traverse point differs from the mean concentration by more than 5.0%.
The gas stream is considered minimally stratified if the concentration at each traverse point differs from the mean concentration by no more than 10%.
